executive.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
executive.description: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong — it's also diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now th

executive.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
executive.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

executive.title: Snap is cutting 1,000 jobs, 16% of its workforce
executive.description: The company cites advancements in AI for the cuts. CEO Evan Spiegel also wrote that the company is closing more than 300 open roles. Snap had about 5,261 full-time employees as of December 2025.

executive.title: Amazon says it is laying off 16,000 employees
executive.description: This is Amazon's second layoff after the company cut 14,000 corporate jobs in October.

executive.title: AI CEOs transformed Davos into a tech conference
executive.description: The World Economic Forum’s annual meeting in Davos felt different this year, and not just because Meta and Salesforce took over storefronts on the main promenade. AI dominated the conv

executive.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap
executive.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from financ

executive.title: Are we tokenmaxxing our way to nowhere?
executive.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from financ

executive.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
executive.description: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

executive.title: From the Startup Battlefield stage to the International Space Station: geCKo Materials built a sticky product
executive.description: This week on Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen speaks with Capella Kerst, founder and CEO of geCKo Materials.

funding.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna’s layoffs and recovery
funding.description: In 2021, Anjuna Security was growing fast, hiring aggressively, and chasing a market that seemed limitless. By the end of that year, the venture-backed cybersecurity company had scaled to around 75 em

funding.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
funding.description: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

funding.title: Vimeo starts layoffs after acquisition by Bending Spoons
funding.description: Vimeo is in the process of laying off some of its global staff after being acquired last year by Bending Spoons for $1.38B.

funding.title: Loop raises $95M to build supply chain AI that predicts disruptions
funding.description: The San Francisco startup closed a Series C funding round led by Antonio Gracias' firm Valor, which is a major backer of xAI.

funding.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
funding.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

funding.title: Slash, a Ramp competitor founded by teenagers, raises $100M at $1.4B valuation
funding.description: Five years later the founders, now 24 years old, say their startup has achieved $300 million in annualized revenue.

funding.title: AI learning app Gizmo levels up with 13M users and a $22M investment
funding.description: Gizmo, an AI-powered learning platform, has attracted more than 13 million users and just secured $22 million in Series A funding.

layoffs.title: Digg lays off staff and shuts down app as company retools
layoffs.description: Digg laid off a significant number of staff and shut down its app, but says it's not giving up on the startup.

layoffs.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
layoffs.description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

layoffs.title: eBay to lay off 800 staff
layoffs.description: The latest round of layoffs marks eBay’s third workforce reduction in the past three years.

layoffs.title: Lucid Motors slashes 12% of its workforce as it seeks profitability
layoffs.description: The layoffs affect hundreds of full-time workers in the US, according to an internal memo obtained by TechCrunch.

layoffs.title: AI layoffs or ‘AI-washing’?
layoffs.description: How many of the companies with recent layoffs are just using AI as an excuse?

layoffs.title: Pinterest layoffs impact 15% of staff as resources redirected to AI
layoffs.description: Pinterest says it's "reallocating resources to AI-focused roles and teams that drive AI adoption and execution" and "prioritizing AI‑powered products and capabilities."
